title: The One with All the Cheesecakes

jokes.text: You have got to try this cheesecake... Oh, you know, I'm not that much of a sweet tooth... [GROANS] Oh, my God, it's so creamy.
jokes.setup: Rachel politely declines the cheesecake, claiming she's not into sweets
jokes.punchline: Immediate groaning and complete reversal after one bite
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/payoff with good structure and economy. The instant conversion works well.

jokes.text: Chandler, this isn't addressed to you. This is addressed to Mrs. Braverman downstairs. [GASPS] Thief.
jokes.setup: Revelation that they're eating stolen cheesecake
jokes.punchline: Rachel's dramatic gasp and accusation
jokes.explanation: Good reveal structure with Rachel's moral outrage providing the comedic beat.

jokes.text: Because it's too delicious.
jokes.setup: Why can't they return the opened cheesecake?
jokes.punchline: Chandler's completely illogical but honest reasoning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler logic - completely self-serving but delivered with such conviction it becomes endearing. High economy and character specificity.

jokes.text: The only losers are the big cheesecake conglomerate: 'Mama's Little Bakery.'
jokes.setup: Chandler trying to justify theft by positioning it as anti-corporate
jokes.punchline: The 'conglomerate' is clearly a small family bakery
jokes.explanation: Excellent irony with Chandler's rationalization completely undermined by the homey bakery name.

jokes.text: I feel terrible. I'm a horrible, horrible, horrible person. Well, I'm sorry, what?
jokes.setup: Rachel's dramatic self-flagellation about the stolen cheesecake
jokes.punchline: Chandler completely tuning out her moral crisis
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Rachel's guilt vs Chandler's complete indifference.

jokes.text: You know how I'm in a coma? Today they do a test on me and it turns out I'm not brain-dead. So... Ah-ah, Mr. Smarty-Pants. It's just my character that's not brain-dead.
jokes.setup: Joey excitedly sharing soap opera news about not being brain-dead
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic correction that forces Joey to clarify it's his character
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey/Chandler dynamic with Joey's excitement about his soap storyline and Chandler's dry correction.

jokes.text: Oh, I have dinner plans with Joey. We get together about once a month to discuss the rest of you guys.
jokes.setup: Casual mention of dinner plans with Joey
jokes.punchline: Revelation that they regularly gossip about their friends
jokes.explanation: Great character insight that feels very true to Phoebe and Joey's friendship dynamic.

jokes.text: Wow. Did not know that. May I say how lovely you look today? Duly noted.
jokes.setup: Ross learning he's being discussed, then awkwardly complimenting Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's completely flat acknowledgment
jokes.explanation: Good economy with Phoebe's deadpan response to Ross's transparent attempt at damage control.

jokes.text: Well, maybe since the age of 9, Frannie's made some new friends.
jokes.setup: Monica lamenting that she and Frannie were inseparable from ages 7-9
jokes.punchline: Ross's dry observation about the obvious passage of time
jokes.explanation: Classic Ross dryness with perfect timing to deflate Monica's nostalgic bubble.

jokes.text: Yeah, well, you call her and tell her that, you know, when we were kids... her precious little Frannie tried to undress me several times, okay? If I hadn't stopped her, there probably wouldn't be a wedding to go to.
jokes.setup: Monica wanting Ross to call about the wedding invitation
jokes.punchline: Revelation of childhood inappropriate behavior escalating to preventing the wedding
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that escalates well with Monica's bitter revelation about childhood incidents.

jokes.text: She tried to undress me too. I used to undress my cousin Glenn.
jokes.setup: Monica's revelation about inappropriate childhood behavior
jokes.punchline: Ross and Chandler casually adding their own similar stories
jokes.explanation: Good escalation but the humor feels a bit forced and relies on shock value.

jokes.text: 'Pheebs, can't make it. Got a date. Talk to you later. Big Daddy.'
jokes.setup: Joey left a note canceling plans
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous 'Big Daddy' signature
jokes.explanation: The 'Big Daddy' nickname is absurd enough to work as character comedy for Joey.

jokes.text: 'Big Daddy'? Oh, that's a nickname we were trying out. Hey, you know what nickname never caught on? 'The Ross-a-tron.'
jokes.setup: Explanation of the Big Daddy nickname
jokes.punchline: Ross's sad attempt to revive his failed nickname
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross character moment - his desperate attempt to make 'The Ross-a-tron' happen.

jokes.text: Whoa, what are you middle-naming me for?
jokes.setup: Phoebe angrily calling him Joseph Francis Tribbiani
jokes.punchline: Joey's confused reaction to being middle-named
jokes.explanation: Good Joey line that captures his childlike confusion about being in trouble.

jokes.text: Hey, you can cancel plans with friends if there's a possibility for sex. Phoebe, he's right. That is the rule.
jokes.setup: Phoebe upset about Joey canceling plans
jokes.punchline: Joey stating the 'rule' with Rachel's immediate agreement
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about unspoken dating rules with good delivery.

jokes.text: How about dinner tomorrow night? I'll pay for myself.
jokes.setup: Joey trying to make amends for canceling
jokes.punchline: His 'generous' offer to pay for himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - thinks he's being generous while offering the bare minimum.

jokes.text: Joe, while you're over there, how about another beer for 'The Ross-a-tron'?
jokes.setup: Ross still trying to make his nickname happen
jokes.punchline: Pathetically working it into casual conversation
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Ross won't let this go, perfect character persistence.

jokes.text: 'Ross...' Is that back?
jokes.setup: Ross using the nickname again
jokes.punchline: Joey's confused question about its status
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey confusion about the social status of Ross's failed nickname.

jokes.text: Well, I've forgotten what it tastes like, okay? It was cheesecake. It was fine.
jokes.setup: Chandler claiming he's forgotten the cheesecake taste
jokes.punchline: Rachel's dismissive description immediately proven wrong
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the detailed description to follow, showing their self-denial.

jokes.text: It had a buttery, crumbly, graham-cracker crust... with a very rich... yet light cream-cheese filling.
jokes.setup: Rachel claiming the cheesecake was just 'fine'
jokes.punchline: Incredibly detailed, sensual description that contradicts her dismissal
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with the contrast between 'fine' and the loving detail.

jokes.text: Wow, my whole mouth just filled with saliva.
jokes.setup: Rachel's sensual description of the cheesecake
jokes.punchline: Chandler's honest physical reaction
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy beat with Chandler's blunt honesty about his reaction.

jokes.text: Where do you wanna go to lunch? Mama's Little Bakery, Chicago, Illinois.
jokes.setup: Trying to distract themselves from cheesecake thoughts
jokes.punchline: Chandler suggesting they go to the actual bakery in Chicago
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback with perfect escalation - Chandler's complete surrender to the obsession.

jokes.text: Mel Tormé died. Joey, that paper's like a year old. Oh, does that mean the Sam Goody's sale is over?
jokes.setup: Joey reading old news about Mel Tormé's death
jokes.punchline: His concern about missing a sale after learning the paper is old
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey priorities - completely missing the gravity of death for shopping concerns.

jokes.text: Limited seating? I am just one tiny person. Yeah, but she doesn't know that. I mean, the last time she saw you... you would've turned one of those little wedding chairs into kindling.
jokes.setup: Monica protesting about limited seating excuse
jokes.punchline: Ross's brutal honesty about her past weight
jokes.explanation: Good sibling cruelty with Ross's matter-of-fact delivery about chair destruction.

jokes.text: Joan and I will just make an appearance... and then we'll leave early as a sign of protest. Joan? Yeah, Joan Tedeschi, my date. She's an assistant professor in the Linguistics Department. Tall, very beautiful. And despite what some people say, not broad-backed.
jokes.setup: Ross describing his sophisticated date
jokes.punchline: Defensive clarification about her back width
jokes.explanation: Excellent Ross character work - the unnecessary defensive detail is perfectly awkward.

jokes.text: Wait a minute. You got 'Ross Geller and guest'? I wasn't invited, and you got 'and guest'?
jokes.setup: Learning Ross can bring someone
jokes.punchline: Monica's mounting outrage at the injustice
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Monica's indignation with realistic sibling jealousy.

jokes.text: She's an assistant professor in the Linguistics Department, okay? They're wild.
jokes.setup: Ross defending why he can't cancel on Joan
jokes.punchline: His absurd characterization of linguistics professors as 'wild'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Ross line - the complete mismatch between linguistics and 'wild' is perfectly nerdy.

jokes.text: Mrs. Braverman must be out. She could be out of town. Maybe she'll be gone for months. By then, the cheesecake may have gone bad. We don't want her to come back to bad cheesecake. That could kill her. Wouldn't want that.
jokes.setup: Seeing another cheesecake delivery
jokes.punchline: Increasingly elaborate rationalization for theft ending in fake concern
jokes.explanation: Good escalating rationalization that builds to absurd conclusion about protecting Mrs. Braverman.

jokes.text: Oh, you say someone's name enough, they turn around.
jokes.setup: David turning around after Phoebe says his name repeatedly
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's matter-of-fact observation about the coincidence
jokes.explanation: Very Phoebe-like pseudo-wisdom that's both logical and absurd.

jokes.text: Did you get a haircut? Yeah. Well, I got like 30 of them.
jokes.setup: Standard 'did you get a haircut' question
jokes.punchline: David's literal interpretation over time passage
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of small talk with David's scientific literalness.

jokes.text: There's a statue in Minsk... that reminds me of you so much. I mean, um, it's actually of Lenin... but, you know, at certain angles...
jokes.setup: Romantic statement about a statue that reminds him of Phoebe
jokes.punchline: It's Lenin, awkwardly qualified with 'certain angles'
jokes.explanation: Excellent awkward romantic gesture that perfectly captures David's scientific awkwardness.

jokes.text: And now, you know, what? Just because, potentially, the love of my life... comes back from Russia just for one night, I should change my beliefs? I should change my beliefs.
jokes.setup: Phoebe wrestling with her principles about not canceling plans
jokes.punchline: Instant reversal after considering it's the 'love of her life'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Phoebe's moral flexibility when romance is involved.

jokes.text: No. No, no. If I don't have my principles, I don't have anything. God, you are so strong. Or I should rush through dinner with Joey and I could meet David at 9.
jokes.setup: Phoebe declaring she must stick to her principles
jokes.punchline: Immediate abandonment for a compromise plan
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Phoebe's principles crumbling under romantic pressure.

jokes.text: Oh, it's, um... It's tofu cake. Do you want some? Aah.
jokes.setup: Joey asking about what Rachel's eating
jokes.punchline: Her lie about tofu cake and Joey's disgusted reaction
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Joey's predictable disgust at healthy food.

jokes.text: I think she's trying to pull a fast one on Big Daddy.
jokes.setup: Learning Phoebe has conflicting plans
jokes.punchline: Joey using his ridiculous nickname seriously
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Joey taking his silly nickname seriously in context.

jokes.text: I'm a relative and I didn't get invited, okay? A blood relative. Blood. Stop saying 'blood' to strangers.
jokes.setup: Monica explaining her connection to the bride
jokes.punchline: Her escalating emphasis on 'blood' and Ross's embarrassed correction
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy with Monica's increasing desperation and Ross's damage control.

jokes.text: Actually, we're out of the lobster ravioli. Oh, well, that changes everything.
jokes.setup: Waiter announcing they're out of lobster ravioli after lengthy menu deliberation
jokes.punchline: Joey's dramatic response to this revelation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey food obsession moment with his dramatic reaction to menu changes.

jokes.text: You know what I really want? Is to have a long, long talk. You know? Get Joey out on the open road and really open him up.
jokes.setup: Phoebe trying to stall dinner while having ulterior motives
jokes.punchline: Her transparent attempt to drag out the meal with fake emotional bonding
jokes.explanation: Good irony with Phoebe's obviously insincere attempt at meaningful conversation.

jokes.text: Okay, well, my girl from the other night was special. She was a scientist too. She was? Well, she graduated high school.
jokes.setup: Joey comparing his date to David, calling her a scientist
jokes.punchline: His definition of 'scientist' being high school graduation
jokes.explanation: Excellent Joey logic with his hilariously low bar for scientific expertise.

jokes.text: I will give you $100 to whistle right now.
jokes.setup: Rachel caught eating cheesecake with her mouth full
jokes.punchline: Chandler's impossible challenge to prove she's not eating
jokes.explanation: Brilliant physical comedy setup with perfect economy and impossible challenge.

jokes.text: We are dessert stealers. We are living outside the law.
jokes.setup: Chandler threatening to tell on Rachel
jokes.punchline: His dramatic characterization of their cheesecake theft as outlaw behavior
jokes.explanation: Great escalation turning petty theft into outlaw drama with good quotability.

jokes.text: Oh, you know what? I think Monica would be very interested to know... that you called her cheesecake dry and mealy.
jokes.setup: Rachel needing leverage in the cheesecake negotiation
jokes.punchline: Her threat about the fake Monica insult
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation but the threat feels somewhat forced and generic.

jokes.text: No sharing, no switching, and don't come crying to me... if you eat your piece too fast. [SHOUTS]
jokes.setup: Chandler's stern rules about the cheesecake division
jokes.punchline: Immediate violation as Rachel presumably dives into her piece
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with immediate payoff after the stern warnings.

jokes.text: Ha, ha. I may just sit here and have my cake all day. Just sit here in the hallway and eat my...
jokes.setup: Chandler gloating about taking his time with the cheesecake
jokes.punchline: Presumably dropping his piece in the hallway
jokes.explanation: Good setup and payoff with Chandler's smugness immediately punished.

jokes.text: Every time, on the lips. Why? Why on the lips?
jokes.setup: Aunt Millie kissing Ross at the wedding
jokes.punchline: His horrified questioning of the inappropriate affection
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy about uncomfortable family affection with relatable horror.

jokes.text: Frannie was the one who found your Playboys and showed them to Mom. That bitch.
jokes.setup: Ross warning Monica to be nice to Frannie
jokes.punchline: Monica's instant vindictive response upon learning the truth
jokes.explanation: Good character work with Monica's immediate shift from conciliation to vengeance.

jokes.text: Stuart. I believe you know my husband. So it's really a question of 'who' could you have possibly done.
jokes.setup: Monica asking what she could have possibly done to Frannie
jokes.punchline: Revelation that Monica slept with Frannie's now-husband
jokes.explanation: Excellent reveal with perfect structure and dramatic irony. Great payoff to the wedding mystery.

jokes.text: That's really beautiful. What does it mean? 'Please clean my beakers.' I don't get out of the lab much.
jokes.setup: David speaking Russian romantically to Phoebe
jokes.punchline: The mundane translation and his awkward explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect David character moment - romantic gesture undermined by scientific reality.

jokes.text: Hey, you could always visit him. Oh, right. Like they're gonna let me have a passport.
jokes.setup: Joey suggesting Phoebe could visit David in Russia
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's assumption she couldn't get a passport
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe paranoia about government institutions with good character specificity.

jokes.text: I could give it a shot.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining complex scientific research needed to bring David back
jokes.punchline: Joey's confident offer to tackle subatomic particle research
jokes.explanation: Excellent Joey moment with perfect economy - his complete lack of understanding masked by confidence.

jokes.text: Oh, look. There's a piece that doesn't have floor on it.
jokes.setup: Cheesecake pieces dropped on hallway floor
jokes.punchline: Rachel's desperate attempt to find salvageable piece
jokes.explanation: Great culmination of the cheesecake obsession with perfect desperation comedy.

jokes.text: Why? Why on the lips?
jokes.setup: Another inappropriate kiss from a relative
jokes.punchline: Ross's repeated horror at lip kissing
jokes.explanation: Good callback that brings the episode full circle with Ross's ongoing trauma.

tmdb_overview: Phoebe gets upset with Joey when he blows off dinner with her for a date, then reverses happen. Chandler and Rachel enjoy a misdirected delicious cheesecake. Ross and Monica attend an awkward cousin wedding.
